spec_match.rationale: Touches focusVisible, focus-visible from the chromestatus summary but skips newly-focused, pseudo-class — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener); controls include "Open menu (or use ArrowDown)"; "Restore focus silently"; "Open file" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link found — the self-heal cleanup script cannot recover this folder if the slug ever drifts.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on menu-focus-trace
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Focus's focusVisible option introduces newly-focused, pseudo-class but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-focusoptions-focusvisible

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on menu-focus-trace
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link to the see-also section
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #3: every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link so the slug-recovery script can recover this folder if the name ever drifts.

summary: menu-focus-trace covers the happy path of Focus's focusVisible option and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
